name: Workmen'S Circle Incorporated
description_short: Nonprofit credit union serving Savannah, Georgia since 1950, offering member-owned banking with mortgages, auto loans, investment property financing, and federally insured savings products.
description_long:
Workmen's Circle Credit Union (WCCU) was established on February 6, 1950, by nine founding members in Savannah, Georgia. The organization traces its roots to the early 1900s when Jewish businessmen formed a financial cooperative to assist Jewish immigrants obtain fair-rate loans when traditional banks denied them credit. This mission of 'people helping people' remains central to WCCU's operations today. The credit union remains locally rooted, serving the Savannah area for over 70 years with deep community ties and institutional stability.

WCCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products across lending and deposit categories. On the lending side, they provide residential and commercial mortgages (fixed-rate up to 30 years), home equity loans and lines of credit, real estate investment loans, auto loans for new or used vehicles, and SBA 504 mortgages. On savings, they offer federally insured savings accounts and money market accounts with above-market rates, CDs in 6/12/24-month terms, and multiple IRA options (Traditional, Roth, and self-directed). They also provide share-secured loans. Recent additions include mobile check deposit functionality via their downloadable app.

WCCU distinguishes itself as a nonprofit cooperative owned entirely by its members with no external stockholders. This structure means all profits and decisions prioritize member benefit rather than shareholder returns. The organization emphasizes personalized service, competitive rates, and low closing costs on home equity products. Their long operational history (since 1950) provides stability and institutional knowledge in a local market. The emphasis on member-first values and cooperative principles differentiates them from commercial banks and larger financial institutions.

A key honest assessment: WCCU is a solid regional credit union with legitimate products and member-focused governance, but their service area is geographically limited to the Savannah, Georgia region. Membership eligibility criteria are not detailed on their website, which is typical for credit unions but may restrict access for those outside their defined field of membership. Their website lacks transparency on APRs, fees, or rate comparisons, requiring prospective members to contact them directly. They lack the technological sophistication or national reach of larger institutions, though this may not concern members prioritizing personal service.
services: Residential mortgage loans (fixed-rate up to 30 years)
SBA 504 mortgages for small business real estate
pros: Nonprofit, member-owned cooperative structure with no shareholders—profits returned to members
Established 1950 with 70+ years of operational history and community stability in Savannah
Federally insured deposits up to $250,000 through NCUA insurance
Comprehensive lending products including SBA 504 mortgages, investment property loans, and home equity lines of credit
Above-market savings rates and money market rates emphasize member value
Low closing costs on home equity products
Mobile check deposit available via app for convenient banking access
cons: Geographic limitation—services restricted to Savannah, Georgia area membership
Membership eligibility criteria not disclosed on website, suggesting restricted field of membership typical of credit unions
No interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules published online; requires direct contact for pricing
Limited digital banking information—no mention of online account management beyond mobile check deposit
Smaller institution means fewer physical branches and less technological infrastructure compared to national banks
best_for: Savannah-area residents seeking member-owned banking with personalized service and competitive rates
Homebuyers and real estate investors needing mortgages and investment property loans from a values-driven lender
Members prioritizing nonprofit cooperative principles and fair lending over maximum convenience or technology features
diagnosis: Workmen's Circle Credit Union is best for Savannah-area residents who value member-owned governance, fair lending practices, and personalized service over digital convenience and competitive rate shopping. The main caveat is that membership appears restricted to specific eligibility criteria (typical for credit unions), and the lack of published pricing means prospective members must contact them directly to compare rates with alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
